{{lowercase title}} {{Infobox software | name = tail | logo = | screenshot = | screenshot size = | caption = | author = | developer = Various [[open-source software|open-source]] and [[commercial software|commercial]] developers | released = | latest release version = | latest release date = | programming language = [[C (programming language)|C]] | operating system = [[Unix]], [[Unix-like]], [[V (operating system)|V]], [[Plan 9 from Bell Labs|Plan 9]], [[Inferno (operating system)|Inferno]], [[MSX-DOS]], [[FreeDOS]] | platform = [[Cross-platform]] | genre = [[Command (computing)|Command]] | license = Plan 9: [[Lucent Public License]] or [[GNU General Public License|GPLv2]] or [[MIT License]]
[[coreutils]]: [[GPLv3+]] | website = }} '''tail''' is a [[Computer program|program]] available on [[Unix]], [[Unix-like]] systems, [[FreeDOS]] and [[MSX-DOS]] used to display the tail end of a text [[Computer file|file]] or [[pipe (computing)|pipe]]d data. ==Options== * +2 ** gives all entries starting with the second entry * -2 ** gives the last two entries ==See also== *[[head (Unix)]] *[[less (Unix)]] *[[List of Unix commands]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Wikibooks|Guide to Unix|Commands}} * [https://www.gnu.org/software/coreutils/manual/html_node/tail-invocation.html GNU Project documentation for tail] * {{man|1|tail|FreeBSD}} * {{man|1|tail|Plan 9}} {{Unix commands}} {{Plan 9 commands}} {{Core Utilities commands}} [[Category:Unix SUS2008 utilities]] [[Category:Unix text processing utilities]] [[Category:Plan 9 commands]] [[Category:Linux]]

[[coreutils]]: [[GPLv3+]] | website = }} '''tail''' is a [[Computer program|program]] available on [[Unix]], [[Unix-like]] systems, [[FreeDOS]] and [[MSX-DOS]] used to display the tail end of a text [[Computer file|file]] or [[pipe (computing)|pipe]]d data. ==Options== * +2 ** gives all entries starting with the second entry * -2 ** gives the last two entries ==See also== *[[head (Unix)]] *[[less (Unix)]] *[[List of Unix commands]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Wikibooks|Guide to Unix|Commands}} * [https://www.gnu.org/software/coreutils/manual/html_node/tail-invocation.html GNU Project documentation for tail] * {{man|1|tail|FreeBSD}} * {{man|1|tail|Plan 9}} {{Unix commands}} {{Plan 9 commands}} {{Core Utilities commands}} [[en:Tail]] {{wikidata link|Q60960}} [[Category:Linux]] [[Category:Plan 9 commands]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages November 4 2025]] [[Category:Unix SUS2008 utilities]] [[Category:Unix text processing utilities]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[coreutils]]: [[GPLv3+]] | website = }} '''tail''' is a [[Computer program|program]] available on [[Unix]], [[Unix-like]] systems, [[FreeDOS]] and [[MSX-DOS]] used to display the tail end of a text [[Computer file|file]] or [[pipe (computing)|pipe]]d data. ==Options== * +2 ** gives all entries starting with the second entry * -2 ** gives the last two entries ==See also== *[[head (Unix)]] *[[less (Unix)]] *[[List of Unix commands]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Wikibooks|Guide to Unix|Commands}} * [https://www.gnu.org/software/coreutils/manual/html_node/tail-invocation.html GNU Project documentation for tail] * {{man|1|tail|FreeBSD}} * {{man|1|tail|Plan 9}} {{Unix commands}} {{Plan 9 commands}} {{Core Utilities commands}} [[en:Tail]] {{wikidata link|Q60960}} [[Category:Linux]] [[Category:Plan 9 commands]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages November 4 2025]] [[Category:Unix SUS2008 utilities]] [[Category:Unix text processing utilities]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[coreutils]]: [[GPLv3+]] | website = }} '''tail''' is a [[Computer program|program]] available on [[Unix]], [[Unix-like]] systems, [[FreeDOS]] and [[MSX-DOS]] used to display the tail end of a text [[Computer file|file]] or [[pipe (computing)|pipe]]d data. ==Options== * +2 ** gives all entries starting with the second entry * -2 ** gives the last two entries ==See also== *[[head (Unix)]] *[[less (Unix)]] *[[List of Unix commands]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Wikibooks|Guide to Unix|Commands}} * [https://www.gnu.org/software/coreutils/manual/html_node/tail-invocation.html GNU Project documentation for tail] * {{man|1|tail|FreeBSD}} * {{man|1|tail|Plan 9}} {{Unix commands}} {{Plan 9 commands}} {{Core Utilities commands}} [[en:Tail]] {{wikidata link|Q60960}} [[Category:Linux]] [[Category:Plan 9 commands]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages November 4 2025]] [[Category:Unix SUS2008 utilities]] [[Category:Unix text processing utilities]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[coreutils]]: [[GPLv3+]] | website = }} '''tail''' is a [[Computer program|program]] available on [[Unix]], [[Unix-like]] systems, [[FreeDOS]] and [[MSX-DOS]] used to display the tail end of a text [[Computer file|file]] or [[pipe (computing)|pipe]]d data. ==Options== * +2 ** gives all entries starting with the second entry * -2 ** gives the last two entries ==See also== *[[head (Unix)]] *[[less (Unix)]] *[[List of Unix commands]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Wikibooks|Guide to Unix|Commands}} * [https://www.gnu.org/software/coreutils/manual/html_node/tail-invocation.html GNU Project documentation for tail] * {{man|1|tail|FreeBSD}} * {{man|1|tail|Plan 9}} {{Unix commands}} {{Plan 9 commands}} {{Core Utilities commands}} [[en:Tail]] {{wikidata link|Q60960}} [[Category:Linux]] [[Category:Plan 9 commands]] [[Category:Unix SUS2008 utilities]] [[Category:Unix text processing utilities]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

[[coreutils]]: [[GPLv3+]] | website = }} '''tail''' is a [[Computer program|program]] available on [[Unix]], [[Unix-like]] systems, [[FreeDOS]] and [[MSX-DOS]] used to display the tail end of a text [[Computer file|file]] or [[pipe (computing)|pipe]]d data. ==Options== * +2 ** gives all entries starting with the second entry * -2 ** gives the last two entries ==See also== *[[head (Unix)]] *[[less (Unix)]] *[[List of Unix commands]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Wikibooks|Guide to Unix|Commands}} * [https://www.gnu.org/software/coreutils/manual/html_node/tail-invocation.html GNU Project documentation for tail] * {{man|1|tail|FreeBSD}} * {{man|1|tail|Plan 9}} {{Unix commands}} {{Plan 9 commands}} {{Core Utilities commands}} [[en:Tail]] {{wikidata link|Q60960}} [[Category:Linux]] [[Category:Plan 9 commands]] [[Category:Unix SUS2008 utilities]] [[Category:Unix text processing utilities]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages]]

[[coreutils]]: [[GPLv3+]] | website = }} '''tail''' is a [[Computer program|program]] available on [[Unix]], [[Unix-like]] systems, [[FreeDOS]] and [[MSX-DOS]] used to display the tail end of a text [[Computer file|file]] or [[pipe (computing)|pipe]]d data. ==Options== * +2 ** gives all entries starting with the second entry * -2 ** gives the last two entries ==See also== *[[head (Unix)]] *[[less (Unix)]] *[[List of Unix commands]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== {{Wikibooks|Guide to Unix|Commands}} * [https://www.gnu.org/software/coreutils/manual/html_node/tail-invocation.html GNU Project documentation for tail] * {{man|1|tail|FreeBSD}} * {{man|1|tail|Plan 9}} {{Unix commands}} {{Plan 9 commands}} {{Core Utilities commands}} [[en:Tail]] {{wikidata link|Q60960}} [[Category:Linux]] [[Category:Plan 9 commands]] [[Category:Unix SUS2008 utilities]] [[Category:Unix text processing utilities]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
